description: People stand and set objects on 'tomae', the lower constructed surface of a room or other occupied space. A floor may be at ground level or raised above another storey. 'muila' (earth) covers soil or ground; 'motha' (foundation) supports the structure beneath its usable surface.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into rounded mid-back 'o'. Close the lips for 'm', open into stressed 'a', and keep the following mid-front 'e' separate across the final hiatus.

semantic_domains.physical: the constructed lower surface that supports bodies and objects
semantic_domains.spatial: the lower usable plane within a room or occupied structure
